headline: Uber cuts jobs while chasing major new markets

Despite the recent May jobs report, which said 172,000 new jobs were added, with over 90,000 ...

headline: SpaceX IPOs on Friday. The 55% rule says don’t chase it.
summary: For much of the past month, Wall Street has been chanting the same three letters: SPCX, the Nasdaq ticker SpaceX intends to use when it begins trading on June 12, 2026. Retail investors have piled into the Destiny Tech100 vehicle, prediction markets have lit up, and r/wallstreetbets has turned a single post titled “$8 to ... SpaceX IPOs on Friday. The 55% rule says don’t chase it.

headline: Uber Lawsuit Over New York Driver Rules Puts Safety Policy In Focus
summary: Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) has filed a lawsuit against New York City to block enforcement of a new law that limits when it can deactivate drivers. The company argues the law threatens rider safety and violates its constitutional rights. The case centers on how much control ride hailing platforms can retain over driver access to their apps. For investors watching NYSE:UBER, this dispute goes to the core of how the company runs its ride hailing marketplace. Uber operates a global platform...

headline: Lyft, Uber sue New York City to block driver retention law
summary: Lyft joined Uber Technologies in suing New York City to block a new law they said would ‌force them to keep bad drivers who threaten public and passenger safety on ‌their platforms.  The Lyft lawsuit was filed late Wednesday in Manhattan federal court, 24 hours after Uber sued.  They also said the ‌law threatened irreparable harm by ⁠undermining their reputation and goodwill while keeping unsafe drivers, including those accused of sexual misconduct, on the road.

content.title: Lyft, Uber sue New York City to block driver retention law
content.summary: Lyft joined Uber Technologies in suing New York City to block a new law they said would ‌force them to keep bad drivers who threaten public and passenger safety on ‌their platforms.  The Lyft lawsuit was filed late Wednesday in Manhattan federal court, 24 hours after Uber sued.  They also said the ‌law threatened irreparable harm by ⁠undermining their reputation and goodwill while keeping unsafe drivers, including those accused of sexual misconduct, on the road.
